description: Happy enough with current arrangement. STR must overcome the inertia of doing nothing. Often exploring out of curiosity rather than genuine intent to change. Highest loss rate of all archetypes. Low conversion without a specific trigger event.
top_fears: STR being more hassle than it is worth
regretting making a change that cannot be undone
top_questions: Why should I change from what I am doing now?
Is it really worth the hassle?
What if it does not work out?
Can I go back to LTL if needed?
what_resonates: concrete income gap — STR net materially higher than LTL
framing as fully managed (no extra work for owner)
specific comparable property examples in their area
what_to_avoid: generic claims about STR being better
ignoring their satisfaction with status quo
ideal_response_pattern: Acknowledge that their current arrangement is fine then quantify exactly what they are leaving on the table then make the change sound zero-effort then give them a no-risk exit
closing_approach: Low-pressure. Leave them with one clear number: how much more they could earn. Let them come back when the trigger hits. Do not chase aggressively.
typical_blockers: does not want the uncertainty of STR
exploring but not committed to change
follow_up_strategy: Quarterly check-in. When LTL tenant gives notice, that is the trigger moment. Flag them for re-engagement at that point.
